summary: Whole-product design-director review over ~188 captured screenshots: parallel forensic readers per surface group + 2026 external-bar research, for synthesis into one design direction + scorecard

reviews.group: Today + logging (multi-day, log sheet, slots, search, portion, edit-entry)
reviews.signature: There IS a recognisable Suppr identity here and it is a genuine asset: a calm, cream-paper canvas (#F7F6F2-ish), a single dominant brand blue (#588CE4), a tri-state calorie ring as the emotional anchor, and dense-but-legible macro rows with tabular numerals. Cropping the logo, the multi-day Today screens (today-2026-05-15/27/28/29) would still read as one product — the ring + green/red state + 8-slot accent palette is a real point of view that already beats MyFitnessPal's cluttered dashboard and Cal AI's generic cards. The problem is the signature DOES NOT survive the tap into the logging flow. The moment you open Log a meal (ix-30) the canvas language holds, but the search-results screen (ix-33) drops to a flat list of hairline-separated rows that looks like a different, more generic app; the portion sheet commit button (ix-34) goes GREEN while every other commit in the flow is BLUE; and the long-press menu (ix-50-alert) is a raw iOS system action sheet bolted onto an otherwise custom bottom-sheet world. So: a strong signature on the home surface, diluted to "competent tracker" the deeper you log. The destination is carrying the ring/blue/cream identity unbroken from Today through every sheet of the logging flow.

topIssues.title: Commit CTA colour is not unified across the logging flow — green in PortionSheet, blue everywhere else
topIssues.why: Verified against HEAD: FoodSearchPanel.tsx:1402 hardcodes the 'Use this' CTA background to Accent.success (#56A775 green), while TodayEditMealModal 'Save changes' (line 282) and the LogSheet/slot selectors all use the canonical primary Accent.primary (#588CE4 blue, 8-slot lock 2026-05-22). Inside ONE logging session a user sees a green commit button on the portion step and a blue commit button on the edit step. Worse, green is ALSO the calorie-ring 'under budget' state and the macro/fiber colour — so green is doing triple duty (primary action + success state + macro hue), which erodes the one thing the ring's green is supposed to mean. This is the single clearest 'two teams' tell in the group.
topIssues.fix: Repaint the PortionSheet 'Use this' CTA to Accent.primary (blue) to match Save changes and the slot selectors. Reserve Accent.success strictly for the calorie-ring state and macro hues. One primary colour for every commit action in the flow. Ship behind isFeatureEnabled per CLAUDE.md. Owner: design-system-enforcer (encode 'commit CTA = Accent.primary' as the rule) + executor (the one-line repaint).

topIssues.title: Long-press meal menu is a raw iOS system action sheet inside an otherwise fully-custom sheet world
topIssues.why: Verified: TodayMealsSection.tsx:716 builds this via a native Alert/action-sheet (Edit / Copy to another day / Share meal / Delete / Cancel) with iOS's default grey stacked buttons and system blue/red. Every other surface in this group — LogSheet (ix-30), PortionSheet (ix-34), Edit-entry (ix-51) — is a branded bottom sheet on cream paper with the blue accent. The system sheet renders the meal header in default SF styling, uses iOS system-red for Delete (not Accent.destructive token), and breaks the cream canvas with a pure-white floating card + dark scrim. It's the most 'stock-component tell' moment in the flow and it sits on the highest-traffic gesture (managing a logged meal).
topIssues.fix: Replace the native action sheet with a branded bottom sheet matching the Edit-entry sheet language (cream surface, blue accent, Accent.destructive for Delete, the same grabber + corner radius). This also unlocks a tappable meal thumbnail/macro preview the native sheet can't render. Owner: ui-product-designer (design the branded meal-actions sheet) → executor.

topIssues.title: Search-results screen drops the card/elevation language to a flat hairline list — reads as a generic tracker
topIssues.why: On entry to Log a meal (ix-30) the rows sit in the calm cream world with thumbnail placeholders and a tabbed (Recent/Library/Saved) header. The instant you type, ix-33 becomes a flat list of 1px-divider rows — no card, no elevation, tiny grey 'per 100g', and a row of pill filters (All/Recents/Custom/Branded/Generic) that is a SECOND segmented-control language different from the 'Recent | Library | Saved meals' tabs one screen earlier. The verified confidence glyph is a small green CheckCircle2 (FoodSearchPanel.tsx:1085) that is nearly invisible at this scale and competes with the green macro 'P' letters. The whole screen is the weakest-crafted surface in the group and it's the one users hit on every single manual log. MacroFactor and Cronometer both give search rows more structure and a clearer confidence signal.
topIssues.fix: Unify the two filter/tab languages into one segmented-control component, give search rows the same soft-elevation card treatment as the rest of the flow (see depth fix below), and make the confidence/verified signal legible (larger glyph or a tinted chip, not a 14px green tick fighting green macro letters). Owner: ui-product-designer + design-system-enforcer.

topIssues.title: No depth model — every card on Today and in the logging flow is a flat fill with a hairline border doing a shadow's job
topIssues.why: Verified in theme/components: the ring card, macro card, meals card, Weekly Insight, Steps & activity, and Activity Bonus blocks (today-2026-05-15-s00/s01/s02) are all white fills separated from the cream background by a single hairline border with NO elevation — shadow tokens exist only on toasts/modals (FirstLogAcknowledgment, Milestone30DayModal), not on the resting cards. Against the 2026 bar (Copilot Money, Oura, MacroFactor all use one soft 6-10% ambient shadow and zero decorative borders), Suppr's surfaces read flat/cheap even though the content is strong. The cream-on-white contrast is so low that on some captures (today-2026-05-31-s02..s05) the card edges nearly vanish. This is a single-root issue: there is no resting-card elevation token.
topIssues.fix: Introduce ONE soft elevation token (e.g. shadowOpacity ~0.06, radius ~12, y-offset ~4, no border) and apply it to every resting card across Today + the logging sheets; delete the hairline borders that are standing in for shadow. This is the highest-leverage depth move and it unifies ~6 card types on Today alone. Owner: design-system-enforcer (the token) → executor (apply + strip borders), flag-gated.

topIssues.title: The win-moment is dead — hitting target / logging produces no celebration, and over-budget red has no motion
topIssues.surface: today-2026-05-28-s00 (red ring), today-2026-05-27-s00 (green ring), ix-34 portion 'If you log this'
topIssues.why: The calorie ring crossing into green-under-budget (today-2026-05-27) or red-over (today-2026-05-28/29) is the single biggest emotional event in the product and from the captures it appears to be a static colour swap with no transition, no number tween, no haptic cue in the frame. The PortionSheet's 'If you log this … 968 kcal left' preview (ix-34) is the perfect pre-commit reinforcement moment and it's rendered as muted grey micro-text in a flat box — it should be the hero of that sheet. On mobile the canonical PressableScale haptic prop ('success'/'confirm') is the obvious lever for the commit + target-hit moments and there's no evidence it's wired on these CTAs. This is engagement design left on the table on the exact surface that drives daily-return.
topIssues.fix: (1) Animate the ring fill + a number count-up on log; (2) fire a PressableScale 'confirm' haptic on every commit CTA (Use this / Save changes / Log usual) and a 'success' haptic + colour pulse the first time the ring tips under-budget for the day; (3) elevate the 'If you log this' delta to a confident, coloured line. Mirror each on web with a 200ms confirming micro-animation + colour pulse (no haptics on web). Owner: ui-product-designer (the moment design) → executor, with sync-enforcer ensuring web parity.

topIssues.title: Inline 'Log usual' / 'Save as a meal' affordances use three different visual treatments on one screen
topIssues.surface: today-2026-05-15-s00 (amber 'Log usual' banner), today-2026-05-15-s01 (lilac 'Log usual Snacks' pill), today-2026-05-29-s01 ('Save Lunch as a meal' plain link)
topIssues.why: Within the Today meals list three sibling quick-actions render in three styles: the Breakfast 'Log usual: Chilli Feta Eggs' is a full-width amber-tinted banner with an X; the Snacks 'Log usual Snacks' is a small lilac rounded pill with an X; and 'Save Lunch as a meal' (today-2026-05-29-s01) is an underline-free centred text link with a bookmark glyph and no container at all. They're the same class of action (a one-tap shortcut on a meal slot) but a user can't learn one pattern. The amber tint also flirts with the over-budget semantic, adding noise.
topIssues.fix: Define ONE quick-action chip component (single tint = primarySoft blue, consistent radius, consistent dismiss affordance) and use it for every inline meal shortcut. Owner: design-system-enforcer + ui-product-designer.

palette: Single dominant brand blue is well-held on Today and Edit-entry (Accent.primary #588CE4) — this is a genuine strength vs the muddy palettes of MFP/Lose It; keep it. The drift is the COMMIT colour: PortionSheet 'Use this' (ix-34) is green #56A775 while Edit-entry 'Save changes' (ix-51) and the slot selectors are blue — green is overloaded as primary-action + ring-success + macro/fiber hue all at once, diluting the ring's meaning (see top issue P1).

Calorie-ring tri-state is CORRECT per carve-out and is a real differentiator — green under (today-2026-05-27-s00), red over (today-2026-05-28/29-s00), brand gradient on 'Start your day' (today-2026-05-30-s00, today-2026-05-31-s01). Do NOT touch. Macro over-budget bars staying amber/orange (today-2026-05-27 carbs/fat) while the ring is green is the carve-out working exactly as designed.

Inline meal-shortcut tints are inconsistent: amber 'Log usual' banner (today-2026-05-15-s00) vs lilac 'Log usual Snacks' pill (s01) vs untinted 'Save as a meal' link (today-2026-05-29-s01) — three colours for one action class. The amber also risks reading as an over-budget warning.

Colour-as-emotion gap: the cream/white surface relationship is so low-contrast that on the Apple-Health-disconnected empty states (today-2026-05-31-s02..s05) and 'Start your day' (today-2026-05-30-s00) the screen reads washed-out and lifeless — the empty/loading states are where a warmer accent or a clearer card edge would carry the brand, and right now they're the palest, most generic frames in the set.

Search-results confidence signal (ix-33) leans on a 14px green CheckCircle2 that competes visually with the green protein 'P' macro letters in the same row — two greens, two meanings, both tiny. Confidence deserves its own legible treatment, not a green that collides with a macro hue.
consistency: Commit-CTA colour drift: green (PortionSheet ix-34) vs blue (Edit-entry ix-51) for the same 'confirm this log' action — verified at FoodSearchPanel.tsx:1402 vs TodayEditMealModal.tsx:282. Root cause: no shared 'primary commit button' component; each sheet styles its own.

Modal language drift: LogSheet (ix-30), PortionSheet (ix-34) and Edit-entry (ix-51) are custom branded bottom sheets, but the meal long-press (ix-50-alert) is a NATIVE iOS action sheet (TodayMealsSection.tsx:716) — two modal grammars, with system-red Delete instead of Accent.destructive.

Segmented-control drift inside ONE flow: 'Recent | Library | Saved meals' underline tabs (ix-30) vs 'All / Recents / Custom / Branded / Generic' rounded filter pills (ix-33) — two different selection components one tap apart. Slot selectors (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner/Snacks) are consistent blue across LogSheet and Edit-entry — that part is correct, keep it.

Card/elevation drift between surfaces: Today cards and the logging sheets are flat hairline-bordered fills, while the search results (ix-33) drop even the card and become a bare divided list — the depth language is not the same screen-to-screen.

Inline quick-action drift: amber banner vs lilac pill vs plain text link for 'Log usual'/'Save as meal' (today-2026-05-15-s00/s01, today-2026-05-29-s01). NOTE — the 9:41 + notch status bar and tab bar are consistent across every capture; that base chrome is solid and should not be flagged.
depth: No resting-card elevation anywhere — every Today card (ring, macros, meals, Weekly Insight, Steps & activity, Activity Bonus in today-2026-05-15-s00/s01/s02) is a flat white fill separated from cream by a single hairline border; shadow tokens exist only on toasts/modals (FirstLogAcknowledgment, Milestone30DayModal), not resting cards. This is the root 'flat/cheap' tell across the group.

Cream-on-white contrast is so low that card edges nearly disappear on several frames (today-2026-05-31-s02..s05, today-2026-05-30-s00) — the hairline-border-as-shadow approach fails exactly where a soft ambient shadow would still read.

Search-results list (ix-33) is the flattest surface in the group: bare rows, 1px dividers, grey 'per 100g' micro-text, empty grey thumbnail placeholders — pure wireframe-coloured-in, no tactility. This is the most-used manual-log surface and it's the least crafted.

The portion-sheet 'If you log this' preview box (ix-34/ix-35) is a flat tinted rectangle of grey micro-text — the most decision-relevant element on the sheet rendered with the least visual weight. Tier read: Today cards = Generic (flat but clean); search results = Prototype; Edit-entry sheet = Good (best-crafted surface, proper grabber + structured inputs). The whole group should be pulled up to the Edit-entry sheet's level with one shared soft-elevation token.
delight: Dead win-moment: the calorie ring tipping into green-under (today-2026-05-27-s00) or red-over (today-2026-05-28/29-s00) is the product's biggest emotional event and appears to be a static colour swap — no ring-fill animation, no number tween, no celebratory pulse. This is the #1 missing delight moment and it's on the retention-critical surface.

Commit actions likely fire no haptic: 'Use this' (ix-34), 'Save changes' (ix-51), and the 'Log usual' shortcuts are all prime PressableScale 'confirm'/'success' haptic moments and there's no evidence in the flow they're wired — a meal log should feel like it landed.

'If you log this … kcal left' (ix-34) is a perfect pre-commit reinforcement and it's buried as muted grey text — it should brighten/animate as the user changes servings to make the consequence felt before they tap.

Over-buzzing risk is low here (the flow is under-celebrated, not over-celebrated) — the fix is additive: confirm-haptic on every commit, success-haptic + colour pulse the first time the ring goes under-budget each day, and a count-up on the ring's LOGGED number. Web has no haptics — mirror each with a 200ms confirming micro-animation + ring colour pulse so the win lands on both platforms (mobile-decisions-apply-to-web).

Empty/first-log states (today-2026-05-30-s00 'Start your day', today-2026-05-31-s01) are correct in copy but emotionally flat — the 'Start your day' gradient ring is the one bright moment and it's static; a subtle breathing/shimmer on the empty ring would invite the first tap without adding noise.

reviews.group: Recipes (Library, Discover, recipe detail, ingredients, edit-recipe, yield editor, recipe-actions menu, cook mode)
reviews.signature: There IS a signature trying to emerge — a warm off-white canvas (#F7F6F1-ish), photo-forward recipe cards, and a consistent macro-icon row (blue protein / amber-orange carbs / magenta fat / green fibre with the leaf glyph) that reads as "Suppr" across Library, Discover and recipe detail. Crop the logo off Library (library-s00) and the macro-icon row + warm canvas would still identify it. BUT the signature dies at the recipe-detail body: ix-41/42 swap the warm photo-led world for clinical pure-white cards, a generic iOS −/+ servings stepper, plain hairline-divided ingredient rows (ix-43) and a flat "Fits your day · ≈50%" line that should be the emotional peak. The result reads as two designers: a confident photo/macro front-of-house (Library + Discover photo cards) and a stock-component back-office (detail body, edit sheet, yield dialog). The detail screen — the surface a viral recipe-import link lands on — is the weakest-signature surface in the whole group, which is backwards for a launch whose viral hook is recipe landing.
palette: One primary is genuinely unified — #588CE4 blue carries every CTA (Log all, Log, Start Cooking, Create, Try again on the error boundary in create-recipe-s00), and the macro-icon row (protein blue / carbs amber-orange #E8721E / fat magenta #DF5EBC / fibre green #56A775) is token-correct and consistent across library-s00, recipe-detail ix-41, and Discover discover-s00. This is the group's strongest lens — do not sand it.

Bookmark glyph means two colours for the same state: on recipe-detail header (ix-41/42) the saved bookmark is success-GREEN (#56A775, confirmed Accent.success at [id].tsx:1835), but on Library cards (library-s00, library-s02) the bookmark badge renders as a blue/dark filled glyph in a white circle. Same 'saved' concept, two palette readings across one tap-depth — the green should not appear only on detail.

The 'Fits your day · ≈50%' verdict on recipe detail (ix-41) is rendered as muted green body text with a hairline check — colour-as-emotion is absent at the single highest-intent moment of the surface (does this recipe fit my macros today). A best-in-class tracker (MacroFactor's fit cues, Cron's target glow) makes the fit % a tinted, weighted moment; here it's the same green as a passive 'verified' tag, so the win reads as metadata, not a payoff.

Macro 'of 101 / of 68 / of 25 / of 15' sub-labels under each tile (ix-41) are a very low-contrast grey on white — sits near or below 4.5:1 for this small a size; this is the same low-contrast secondary-grey class seen across the detail body (the 'breakfast' meal-type label, 'You' author label on cards). Root-cause the secondary-text token, don't patch per line.

Discover dense-list rows (discover-s03..s06) print macros as monochrome grey '480 · 14P · 50C' with NO macro colour at all — the colour language that defines Library/photo-Discover evaporates the moment the layout switches to a list, so the same data wears two completely different palettes inside one tab.
consistency: DEPTH MODEL SPLIT (root cause, two surfaces): Library photo cards use a hairline border (library.tsx:309 borderWidth:1 / borderColor:colors.border, no shadow) while recipe-detail cards use a real drop shadow (recipe/[id].tsx:1538 shadowColor #000 / shadowOpacity 0.22 / shadowRadius 10). Within one feature group, cards are bordered in one place and shadowed in another — the classic 'two teams' tell. Pick ONE elevation model.

DISCOVER HAS TWO CARD LAYOUTS in one tab: discover-s00..s02 are large photo cards with coloured macros; discover-s03..s06 are dense thumbnail list rows with monochrome macros and a different divider. There is no visible toggle explaining the switch — it reads as an unfinished migration, not an intentional density control.

BOOKMARK BADGE drifts: white-circle-with-blue-bookmark on most Library cards (library-s00) vs a filled-blue-circle bookmark on the Pear/Shrimp cards (library-s02) vs green bookmark in the detail header (ix-41). Three treatments of one control.

SERVINGS CONTROL drifts three ways for the same action: recipe-detail inline 'Servings − 5 +' uses bordered square steppers + a pencil (ix-41); the yield-editor dialog (ix-65) uses a plain text field '5' with Cancel/Save; the edit-recipe sheet (ix-54) uses circular −/+ buttons. Same concept, three component vocabularies.

MACRO PILL ICONS differ between Library cards and Discover photo cards: Library (library-s00) shows flame+drumstick+chilli+leaf glyphs; Discover (discover-s00) shows a different P/C/F/g letter-suffix treatment ('13g P / 18g C'). The macro chip is a core Suppr primitive and it is not the same component across the two sub-tabs of the same screen.

EDIT-RECIPE SHEET (ix-54) and YIELD DIALOG (ix-65) use grey/beige inset input fields (#ECEAE3-ish) that appear NOWHERE else in the group — the rest of the surface is white cards on warm canvas. These two modals look imported from a different design system (stock form styling), with red trash icons that are the only destructive-red in the flow.

The header action row drifts: recipe detail (ix-41) shows back / title / bookmark / share / overflow as four separate top-bar glyphs, while Library (library-s00) cards carry overflow+bookmark INSIDE the card. Acceptable per-context, but the share glyph uses a different (Android-style node) icon than the rest of the lucide set — verify it's the exact prototype glyph.
depth: Recipe-detail elevation is a HARD default-ish shadow, not a crafted one: shadowColor #000 at 0.22 opacity, radius 10, offset y2 (recipe/[id].tsx:1538-1541). At 22% black this reads heavier and greyer than the soft ~8% ambient elevation premium apps use — it's the 'I added a shadow' look, not a considered light source. Surfaces affected: the macro-tile card, the 'Log to journal' card (ix-42), and 'Your notes' card all carry this same heavy shadow.

Library + Discover photo cards are FLAT — they lean on a 1px hairline border (library.tsx borderColor:colors.border) to fake separation. Border-as-shadow is the cheap tell; on the warm canvas these cards have no lift at all, so the beautiful food photography sits in a flat document rather than on tactile cards.

Net result is an inverted depth hierarchy: the photo cards (the things you most want to feel touchable, the viral hook) are flat-bordered, while the clinical detail-body cards (data) carry the only real shadow. Depth is applied to the least emotional content.

Edit sheet (ix-54) and yield dialog (ix-65) inputs are flat beige fills with no inner shadow or focus elevation — they read as wireframe-coloured-in, the lowest-craft surfaces in the group. The yield dialog also dims the background to a flat grey with no blur/scrim gradient, so the modal floats on a muddy backdrop rather than a crafted scrim.

Macro tiles (ix-41) have a thin coloured underline bar under each tile — a nice touch — but the tiles themselves are bordered AND shadowed, double-encoding separation, which makes them feel busier/cheaper than a single clean elevation would.

The 'Shrimp Rice Paper Rolls' fallback (library-s02) renders a brown gradient block with a centered wheat glyph — the empty-image state is a flat muddy brown that looks like a loading error, not a designed placeholder; it cheapens the otherwise photo-rich grid.
delight: DEAD WIN MOMENT (highest-severity delight gap): recipe-detail 'Log all · 458 kcal' and 'Log' fire ZERO haptic — recipe/[id].tsx imports no haptics at all (confirmed: no expo-haptics import in the 3,284-line file). Logging a whole recipe to your day is a top-3 commit-action in the product and it lands silently. Meanwhile cook.tsx is richly hapticised (Haptics.notificationAsync Success on step-complete at cook.tsx:464/542/709/815, selectionAsync on step nav). So the app KNOWS how to celebrate — it just doesn't on the recipe surface. Add a 'confirm' (or 'success' on a full Log all) haptic; web analog = a 200ms colour-pulse / check-morph on the button.

The 'Fits your day · ≈50%' verdict (ix-41) is the surface's signature moment and it's inert — no motion, no tint weight, no haptic when it resolves to a strong fit. A 90%+ fit should feel like a small reward (success haptic + brief green glow on the chip) to reinforce 'pick recipes that fit my macros'. As-is it reinforces nothing.

'Start Cooking' (ix-42) hands off to a cook mode that IS delightful (Success haptic on finishing the last step) — but the transition INTO it fires nothing, so the most satisfying flow in the group has a flat on-ramp. A 'confirm' haptic + a forward push-transition would set up the payoff.

Library/Discover card taps and the bookmark/save toggle use no haptic (library.tsx + recipe card components have no PressableScale/Haptics) — saving a recipe is a collect/dopamine action competing with Paprika/Recime; a light 'selection' tick on save would make the library feel alive. Web analog = the bookmark fills with a quick scale-bounce.

Servings stepper (ix-41) and the 0.5×/1×/1.5×/2× portion chips (ix-42) change real macro numbers on tap with no number-tween and (on mobile) no selection haptic — the macros just snap. Premium trackers tween the number; here the most interactive control updates silently.

The error boundary (create-recipe-s00, 'Something went wrong') and the muddy image-fallback (library-s02) are the un-delighted edges — neither has any warmth or recovery motion; the error screen is a stark near-black void that breaks the warm-canvas identity entirely (it's the only true-dark surface in the group and it doesn't match either the mobile #0a0a0f or web #101014 documented dark tones).

topIssues.title: Recipe-detail commit actions fire no haptic — silent Log all / Log / Start Cooking
topIssues.why: Logging a recipe to the journal is a top-tier commit-action and the recipe-detail file imports zero haptics, while cook.tsx is fully hapticised. The single most-used button on the viral-landing surface lands dead, on the exact screen a shared recipe link opens to. This is a whole-group engagement leak, not a one-screen nit — same dead-moment pattern as Library save taps.
topIssues.fix: Wire PressableScale's haptic prop into the recipe-detail CTAs: 'confirm' on Log / portion-chip / servings steps, 'success' on Log all (whole-recipe commit). Web analog: 200ms colour-pulse + check-morph on the button. Owner: ui-product-designer to spec the moment, design-system-enforcer to make 'every commit-action confirms' a token-level rule, sync-enforcer for web parity. Ship behind isFeatureEnabled.

topIssues.title: Two depth models inside one feature group (bordered cards vs shadowed cards)
topIssues.why: Library/Discover photo cards use a 1px hairline border with no shadow (library.tsx:309); recipe-detail cards use a heavy #000@0.22 drop shadow (recipe/[id].tsx:1538). One feature reads as two teams, and the inversion is wrong-way-round: the emotional photo cards are flat while the clinical data cards get the lift. This is the clearest 'pile of screens that share a logo' tell in the group.
topIssues.fix: Adopt ONE soft elevation token (~6-8% black, larger radius, no border) and apply it to both the photo cards and the detail cards; delete the hairline borders and drop the 0.22 shadow to the soft token. Owner: design-system-enforcer encodes a single `Shadow.card`; executor sweeps both surfaces. Flag-gated.

topIssues.title: 'Fits your day' win-moment is rendered as flat metadata, not a payoff
topIssues.why: The macro-fit verdict is the north-star 'does this fit me today' moment and the differentiator vs Paprika/Recime — but it's the same muted green as a passive 'Verified' tag, with no weight, tint, motion or haptic. The product's best idea is dressed as the least important text on the screen. (Calorie-ring red carve-out respected — this is the fit chip, not the ring.)
topIssues.fix: Promote the fit verdict to a tinted, weighted chip; scale tint/colour with fit quality (strong fit = saturated success green + brief glow + success haptic on resolve). Mirror on web with a colour-pulse. Owner: ui-product-designer; sync-enforcer for parity.

topIssues.title: Edit-recipe sheet + yield dialog look imported from a different design system
topIssues.why: Both modals use flat beige inset inputs (#ECEAE3-ish), a stock −/+ vocabulary, and red trash icons that appear nowhere else in the group, on a muddy un-blurred scrim. Against the warm photo-led identity these are the lowest-craft surfaces and break the signature the moment a user edits anything. Two surfaces share the cause (no shared form/modal primitive).
topIssues.fix: Build one tokenised form-field + modal primitive (warm card surface, soft elevation, blurred scrim) and reuse it for edit sheet, yield dialog and the create-recipe wizard. Owner: ui-product-designer + design-system-enforcer.

topIssues.title: Discover renders the same data in two unreconciled layouts with two palettes
topIssues.surface: Discover (discover-s00..s02 photo cards vs discover-s03..s06 list rows)
topIssues.why: Within one tab, recipes appear as coloured-macro photo cards AND as monochrome-macro dense list rows, with no visible density toggle. The macro colour language — a core Suppr primitive — vanishes in the list view, so one screen wears two palettes. Reads as an unfinished migration.
topIssues.fix: Pick one Discover layout (or add an explicit, designed density toggle) and carry the coloured macro chip into both. Owner: ui-product-designer; design-system-enforcer to make the macro chip a single shared component used in every layout.

topIssues.title: 'Saved' bookmark uses three different colours/treatments across one tap-depth
topIssues.why: The saved bookmark is green on detail, blue-in-white-circle on most Library cards, and filled-blue-circle on others. Same state, three readings — colour stops meaning one thing, which undercuts the otherwise-strong unified palette.
topIssues.fix: One saved-state token + one badge component everywhere (recommend the success-green saved fill, with a light selection haptic + scale-bounce on toggle). Owner: design-system-enforcer; executor sweep.

topIssues.title: Secondary-text token sits at/below 4.5:1 across the detail body
topIssues.surface: Recipe detail macro sub-labels 'of 101/68/25/15' + 'breakfast' + card 'You' author labels (ix-41, library-s00)
topIssues.why: The same low-contrast grey-on-warm-white appears on multiple secondary labels group-wide — a token-level contrast class, not isolated. Forensically these read borderline for their small size; this is exactly the 4.0-vs-4.5 class that thumbnail-eyeballing misses.
topIssues.fix: Darken the secondary-text token to clear 4.5:1 on the warm canvas and re-run the contrast audit (tests/e2e/verify/contrast-audit.spec.ts model) across every recipe surface. Owner: design-system-enforcer; verify via executor.

topIssues.title: Image-fallback + error states break the warm identity
topIssues.surface: Shrimp Rice Paper Rolls fallback (library-s02), create-recipe error boundary (create-recipe-s00)
topIssues.why: The no-photo fallback is a muddy brown gradient that looks like a load failure, and the error boundary is a stark near-black void that matches neither the warm canvas nor the documented dark tones — the only true-dark surface in the group. Both are un-designed edges that cheapen an otherwise photo-rich, warm surface.
topIssues.fix: Design a branded no-image placeholder (warm canvas + tinted macro-coloured glyph, not muddy brown) and bring the error boundary onto the warm-canvas identity with a recovery illustration. Owner: ui-product-designer.

topIssues.title: COVERAGE GAP — cook mode, create-recipe, individual ingredient + ingredient-info-alert never rendered
topIssues.why: ix-75/ix-76 both captured the recipe-detail 'Start Cooking' scroll position, not cook mode; create-recipe-s00 captured the global error boundary, not the form; ix-44 and ix-52 both captured the ingredients LIST, not an ingredient detail or an alert. So four named surfaces in this group are UNCOVERED. cook.tsx and create-recipe.tsx exist in code (verified at HEAD cda3c925) and cook.tsx is the most hapticised file in the group — it must not be judged blind. Declaring this wall 'complete' would be a silent cap.
topIssues.fix: Re-run the tour with deterministic navigation into cook mode, the create-recipe wizard, an individual ingredient row, and the ingredient-info alert, then re-review those four surfaces before the whole-product synthesis treats this group as fully covered. Owner: executor to fix the tour script; re-route to design-director after capture.

reviews.signature: The Plan group reads as the same calm, data-forward Suppr you meet on Today — same cream-white surface (#fdfdf7-ish), same macro token map (protein #588CE4 / carbs #E8721E / fat #DF5EBC / fibre #56A775), same slot-tint glyph language carried faithfully into the Move-meal sheet, same Accent.primary blue on the one primary action per screen. The signature IS present and it IS carried: long-press the meal, the slot tints follow you into the sheet; the macro pill is the same compact P/C/F/Fi delta strip you see app-wide. Cropping the logo, you'd say 'Suppr'. What stops it short of Premium is that the signature is expressed almost entirely in COLOUR and almost not at all in MATERIAL — every surface in this group is a flat fill with a 1px hairline border doing a shadow's job, so the screen reads like a well-coloured wireframe rather than a crafted, layered object. The identity is consistent; the craft is one generation behind the depth model the rest of the product is already moving toward (the Move-meal sheet, notably, has the right soft-shadow elevation — proof the better model already exists in-house and just isn't applied to the tab body).
palette: ix-70-plan-tab.png: macro pill uses the canonical token map correctly — protein #588CE4, carbs #E8721E, fat #DF5EBC, fibre #56A775 — same as Today, so colour-as-meaning holds across surfaces. No drift here; this is a strength to preserve, not sand off.

ix-70-plan-tab.png + planner-s00.png: the over/under delta colours on the pill are inconsistent emotionally. Populated day shows 'Fi 0g +13' in green (good = surplus) while empty days show '-101 -68 -25 -15' where each macro number renders in its own macro hue, not in a deficit colour. The reader can't tell at a glance 'am I short?' — the minus sign is the only signal and it's tiny grey. A premium tracker (MacroFactor) tints the whole delta amber when you're materially short. Colour is decorative here, not diagnostic.

ix-70-plan-tab.png: the header summary 'Hits your targets 0 of 7 days' is the emotional core of the Plan tab and it is rendered in flat near-black body text with zero colour weight. 0/7 is a bad number; it should carry a calm amber or a subtle progress treatment so the user feels the gap and the 'Generate'/'Adjust constraints' CTAs feel like the answer. Right now the headline that should pull the user to act is the least coloured thing on the card.

planner-s00.png / s01.png / s02.png: the empty-week 'No slots yet — add one or regenerate' rows are entirely greyscale (grey text, grey-bordered chips). This is the cold-open state for a user who hasn't built a plan, and it has zero emotional pull or brand colour — it reads as a disabled/loading state rather than an invitation. The only colour on the whole screen is the macro pill numbers, which are deltas against an empty plan (so they're alarmingly negative). First impression of an empty plan is 'broken', not 'let's build this'.

ix-73-move-day-picker.png: the FROM source card is tinted with Accent.primary soft (light blue wash) and a blue border — correct, this is the one place a selection-state colour earns its keep and it's done well.

CROSS-SURFACE: the recipe-404 page that 5 of the tour captures landed on (ix-70-plan.png etc.) uses a more lavender/desaturated primary on its 'Back to Today' button than the Plan tab's saturated #588CE4 'Generate' button — but that is a different surface (recipe deep-link 404), route to ui-critic, not a Plan finding.
consistency: DEPTH MODEL DRIFT (the root finding): within this ONE group there are two elevation languages. The Move-meal sheet (ix-73, ix-72) uses a real soft shadow (shadowOpacity 0.18, radius 24, offset y-8) — crafted. The Plan tab body (ix-70-plan-tab, planner-s00/01/02) uses borderWidth:1 hairline borders on every card, chip, and row with effectively no shadow (the header card's only shadow is opacity 0.06 — invisible). So the modal layer is premium and the tab layer is flat, in the same flow. Same team, two depth models.
Radius is consistent within the group (Radius.lg ~12-16 on cards/rows, Radius.md on chips, Radius.xl on the sheet top corners) — no drift to flag.

Button shape consistency: 'Generate' is a filled primary pill, 'Adjust constraints' is a bordered secondary with a dotted/sliders glyph — this two-button pattern is clean and matches the Today CTA grammar. Good.
Icon set: the slot glyphs (breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack tints + lucide-style glyphs) carry correctly from the action sheet into the Move-meal sheet — exact-icon rule respected.

The filter row '7 days · Today' / 'All meals' / 'Generate' uses three different control treatments side by side (two grey dropdown chips + a blue text-button with a caret). The blue inline 'Generate' next to the big filled 'Generate' button in the header card above it is a duplicated affordance with two different visual weights for the same word — mild confusion, worth unifying.

Action sheet (ix-71-plan-meal-longpress / ix-72): custom on-brand bottom sheet (ActionRow list) rather than native iOS ActionSheet — correct and consistent with Suppr's sheet language. 'Remove from plan' in destructive red is correct usage (not the calorie-ring carve-out, a genuine destructive action).
depth: ROOT: hairline-border-as-shadow across the entire Plan tab body. ix-70-plan-tab.png — the header 'meal plan' summary card, every meal row (Chicken Frittata, Chicken Meatballs, etc.), the '0.5x portion' chips, and the three filter dropdowns are all flat fills outlined by a 1px border. There is no light source, no layering, no tactility. This is the single biggest thing standing between this group and Premium. The fix is one elevation model applied at the card/row component: replace the 1px border with a soft 6-8% shadow (the header card already has shadowOpacity:0.06 — push it to the Move-meal sheet's calibre and drop the borders).

planner-s00/s01/s02.png — the empty-state 'No slots yet' rows and the '+ Bfast / + Lunch / + Dinner / + Snack' chips are flat grey-bordered rectangles. They read as disabled. With no depth and no colour, the entire empty week looks like a skeleton loader that never finished, not an interactive surface inviting a tap.

ix-73-move-day-picker.png — this sheet is the crafted reference within the group: real soft shadow on the sheet, the FROM card lifted with a tinted fill + blue border, list rows with slot-tinted glyph chips. This is the depth bar the Plan tab body should be held to. It proves the better model is already in the codebase (MoveMealSheet.tsx, shadowOpacity 0.18) and simply isn't applied to the tab.

The meal-row thumbnails in ix-70-plan-tab.png are small rounded photos but two rows (Homemade Cream Cheese, and the empty/placeholder) show a flat pale-blue placeholder block with no image — a real-data gap that makes the list look half-loaded. The placeholder needs a branded fallback (slot-tinted icon tile), not a blank pale-blue square, or the list reads as broken.
No hover/press affordance visible in the static captures, but combined with flat borders the rows give no sense they are tappable objects — depth would do double duty as the tappability cue.
reviews.motion: ["Long-press to open the meal action sheet fires a Medium impact haptic (planner.tsx:3008) — correct weight for a context-menu invocation, and it's the right primitive. Good.","A Light impact haptic fires on a secondary plan interaction (planner.tsx:3268). Reasonable.","The action sheet (ix-72) and Move-meal sheet (ix-73) are standard bottom-sheet slide-ups — motion is present and conventional; can't assess easing/spring from stills, but no jank tells in the captures.","GAP — no haptic or motion confirmation on the actual move-completion. The whole point of the Move-meal sheet is committing a meal to a new day/slot; that commit-action is the win moment and there's no evidence of a success/confirm haptic or a settle animation when the meal lands in its new slot. A move that just silently dismisses the sheet reads as a 'refresh', not a confirmation. This is the same class of dead-win-moment the director-level synthesis should root-cause across commit actions.","GAP — 'Generate' (building the whole week's plan) is the single highest-value action on this tab and there is no celebratory motion/haptic payoff when a plan materialises. Plan-generation filling an empty week is a genuine win that should land with a success haptic + a staggered row reveal, not a static re-render."]
delight: The win-moment ledger for this group is mostly empty. Move-meal commit: no success haptic, no settle animation (the sheet's slot tints are a nice touch but the payoff is missing). Plan-generate from empty week: no celebration, no staggered reveal — the screen just blinks from grey skeleton-looking rows to populated. Hitting '7 of 7 days on target' would be the emotional peak of this tab and there is no evidence it's marked at all (the captures show 0/7, but the success path appears unstyled).

The 'Hits your targets N of 7 days' headline is the built-in delight hook and it's rendered as flat text. On a good day it should glow/colour-pulse; on a 0/7 day it should calmly point at the fix. Right now it's emotionally inert in both directions.

Empty-state delight gap: planner-s00 is a user's first meeting with the Plan tab. A premium app turns the empty week into an invitation (warm illustration, a single confident 'Generate my week' CTA with a colour pull). Suppr shows seven identical grey 'No slots yet' rows with negative macro deltas — the opposite of inviting. This is the highest-leverage delight fix in the group because it's a cold-open surface.

Web analog owed: per the project's mobile-decisions-apply-to-web rule and the ENG-699 move-meal-to-web convergence, every haptic win recommended here needs a motion/colour analog on web /planner (a confirming micro-animation on move-commit, a colour pulse on target-hit). Web /planner was not captured in this group, so parity is unverified — flag as uncovered.

topIssues.title: Plan tab body uses hairline-border-as-shadow while its own modals use real elevation — two depth models in one flow
topIssues.why: Every card, row and chip on the Plan tab is a flat fill outlined by a 1px border (header card shadow is only 0.06 opacity = invisible), so the tab reads as a coloured wireframe. The Move-meal sheet in the same flow uses a proper soft shadow (0.18 / radius 24). The product looks like two teams built one feature. This is the single biggest gap to Premium and it's a class-of-bug, not one card.
topIssues.fix: Adopt one elevation model at the card/row component level: replace borderWidth:1 with a soft ~6-8% shadow matching the Move-meal sheet's calibre, drop the decorative borders. The better model already exists in MoveMealSheet.tsx — apply it to the tab. Ship behind a feature flag. Owner: ui-product-designer to spec, design-system-enforcer to encode as the shared card token.

topIssues.title: Empty-week state reads as a broken skeleton, not an invitation
topIssues.why: A user's first Plan tab is seven identical greyscale 'No slots yet — add one or regenerate' rows, each topped by an alarming negative macro pill (-101 -68 -25 -15). Zero brand colour, zero depth, zero warmth. This cold-open surface looks like a loading state that never finished — the opposite of the confident 'let's build your week' moment a premium planner earns.
topIssues.fix: Redesign the empty week as an invitation: one confident primary 'Generate my week' moment with colour pull, suppress the negative-delta pills when the plan is empty (a deficit against nothing is noise, not signal), and give the slot-add chips real surface depth. Owner: ui-product-designer.

topIssues.title: The 'Hits your targets 0 of 7' headline — the tab's emotional core — is rendered as inert flat text
topIssues.why: This summary line is the Plan tab's built-in win/diagnostic hook and it carries no colour weight in either direction. On a good day it should celebrate; on a 0/7 day it should calmly steer the user to Generate/Adjust. As flat near-black text it does neither — the least-coloured element is the one that should drive the user's next action.
topIssues.fix: Give the headline a state-aware treatment: calm amber/progress framing when off-target, a success-green colour pulse + (mobile) success haptic when the week hits 7/7. Mirror the colour pulse on web /planner. Owner: ui-product-designer.

topIssues.title: Move-meal commit and Plan-generate have no win-moment payoff (no success haptic, no settle/reveal motion)
topIssues.why: The two highest-value actions on this tab — committing a meal to a new day, and generating a whole week — both appear to complete silently. Long-press correctly fires a Medium haptic, but the actual commit fires nothing, so a successful move reads as a 'refresh' rather than a confirmation (trust erosion), and a generated plan blinks in with no celebration. Dead win-moments on the exact actions that build the planning habit.
topIssues.fix: Add a success/confirm haptic on move-commit and on plan-generate, plus a settle animation (meal lands in its new slot) and a staggered row reveal on generate. Provide the web motion/colour analog on /planner. Owner: ui-product-designer + sync-enforcer for parity.

topIssues.title: Empty meal-row thumbnail renders as a blank pale-blue square
topIssues.surface: ix-70-plan-tab.png (Homemade Cream Cheese row / placeholder rows)
topIssues.why: Rows without a recipe photo show a flat pale-blue block, making the populated list look half-loaded against the rows that do have thumbnails. A blank coloured square is a wireframe tell.
topIssues.fix: Use a branded fallback tile (slot-tinted glyph on a tinted ground, reusing the existing slot-tint system) instead of an empty colour block. Owner: executor / visual-qa.

topIssues.title: Capture-tour failure: 5 of 13 Plan-group captures are the recipe-404 page, and web /planner is uncovered
topIssues.surface: ix-70-plan.png, ix-71-move-after-longpress.png, ix-71-move-meal-menu.png, plan-s00.png, plan-s01.png (all render the 404 'We couldn''t find that' page); web /planner (no capture)
topIssues.why: The Maestro move-meal deep-link navigation failed and dumped 5 captures onto the recipe-404 screen, so a third of this group's 'evidence' is a dead surface. The real move-meal flow was salvaged by ix-72/ix-73, but any synthesis reading filenames (e.g. 'move-meal-menu') would mistake a 404 for the menu. Web /planner has zero captures in this group, so the ENG-699 move-meal-to-web convergence and all web depth/motion parity is unverified.
topIssues.fix: Fix the tour's move-meal navigation step (it's hitting a stale/invalid recipe deep-link → 404) and add a web /planner pass to the visual sweep so cross-platform parity can actually be judged. Mark web /planner UNCOVERED in the coverage ledger this run. Owner: executor (tour script) + sync-enforcer (web parity capture).

reviews.group: Progress + weight tracker + metric details (mobile / iOS)
reviews.signature: There IS a recognisable Suppr signature on the Progress tab itself (progress-s00–s04): calm cream canvas #F7F6F2, a confident point-of-view that treats progress as a narrative not a dashboard ("Maintenance held steady this week", "Sundays ran higher than Fridays"), the adaptive-maintenance card, the sparse weight chart with the floating "54.7 kg · Thu" callout, and named win-language. Crop the logo from progress-s00/s03 and a designer would recognise a distinct, mature product — this is genuinely a differentiator and the project context is right to defend it. BUT the signature COLLAPSES the moment you leave the tab. The metric-detail screens (metric-calories-s00, metric-protein-s00) and burn-detail (burn-detail-s00) read as a different, blunter app: a shouty saturated-blue ALL-CAPS letter-spaced-2 header, flat hairline-bordered cards instead of the soft-shadow cards of the parent tab, and a near-empty burn screen. The weight-tracker stack (weight-tracker-s00/s01, ix-55/56/57) introduces a THIRD visual register — a solid #588CE4 primary-button "Save", segmented chips with blue selected fill, a brighter chart blue — that doesn't match the muted, restrained Progress hero one tap behind it. So the read is: one obsessive designer built the Progress hero; the detail/sub-screens were built by someone working from a wireframe. The signature exists but is not carried past the first scroll.

topIssues.title: Detail-screen cards are flat hairline-bordered while the parent Progress tab cards have real soft elevation — depth model breaks on tap-through
topIssues.why: Confirmed in source against HEAD: Progress tab cards (progress.tsx) use shadowColor #000 / shadowOpacity 0.1 / elevation, but progress-metric.tsx and burn-detail.tsx use only borderWidth:1 + borderColor (cardBorder) with NO shadow. Visually, tapping the Calories card on progress-s00 drops the user from soft-floating cards into flat bordered boxes — the classic 'border doing a shadow's job' cheap tell, and it reads as two different teams within one flow. The list rows on metric-calories-s00 are especially flat: stacked white rectangles separated by faint borders, no layering, wireframe-coloured-in.
topIssues.fix: Apply ONE elevation model across the whole group: the Progress tab's soft 8–10% shadow (no hairline border) on every card in progress-metric.tsx and burn-detail.tsx. Delete the borderWidth:1 borders that are standing in for depth. This is a token-level fix in the two detail files, not 15 call-sites.

topIssues.title: Metric-detail header is a shouty saturated-blue ALL-CAPS letter-spaced banner that exists nowhere else in Suppr
topIssues.why: Confirmed from source: progress-metric.tsx renders title.toUpperCase() at fontSize 18, fontWeight 800, letterSpacing 2, color Accent.primary (#588CE4). The Progress tab itself uses a calm near-black 'Progress' title and sentence-case card headers ('Maintenance held steady this week'). This big blue all-caps header is a different typographic voice — it reads like a 2018 dashboard section-label, not the 2026 calm-narrative voice of the parent. It also fights the back-chevron and gives the screen a loud, generic feel the rest of the surface group earns its way out of.
topIssues.fix: Replace with Suppr's actual title voice: near-black (colors.text) sentence-case at the Progress-tab title weight, left-aligned under the back chevron — e.g. 'Calories this week' / 'Protein consistency' as a real H1, not a colour-shouted label. Keep blue strictly for the data/accent, not the chrome.

topIssues.title: Weight-tracker stack is a third visual register — brighter blue, solid primary button, chip fills — that doesn't match the muted Progress hero
topIssues.why: The Weight & Trends screen uses a noticeably brighter/more-saturated blue than the Progress hero: the selected segmented chip ('3M'), the selected '12 mo' import-depth chip, the chart line, and a solid blue 'Save' button. Set this beside progress-s03's weight card (same data, muted blue line, no chrome) and they look like two different products' weight features. The 'Save' input pill is a flat grey field with a blue button — a stock form pattern with no relationship to the crafted callout-on-chart treatment one tab away. The two screens answer the same question (weight trend) with two different design languages.
topIssues.fix: Reconcile to one blue and one chart language: use the Progress-hero muted chart blue and callout style on Weight & Trends; make the segmented control + import-depth chips match whatever chip pattern Progress/Today use; restyle the Save row to the product's elevated-card input pattern rather than a stock grey-field+blue-button.

topIssues.title: Win moments across the entire group are dead — no haptic, no celebratory colour/motion on the app's biggest progress payoffs
topIssues.surface: ix-57-log-weight-sheet (Save weight), metric-protein-s00 (4/7 on target, 116g best day), progress-s00 (11-day streak), progress-s01 (Share week)
topIssues.why: This surface group is where the product should FEEL its wins, and every one is silent. Logging a weight (ix-57) re-learns your TDEE — a genuine moment — yet the Save flow shows no success state, no haptic implied, no number tween. The protein detail (metric-protein-s00) proudly says '4/7 on target' and shows a 116g best day, but renders it as plain bold text with no warmth or celebration. The 11-day streak on progress-s00 is buried as grey dot-separated metadata next to '154% Over target', so the win is overshadowed by the over-target frame. None of these fire the PressableScale success haptic that the product already has as a primitive. The web has no motion analog either.
topIssues.fix: Wire the existing PressableScale 'success' haptic + a 200ms number/colour pulse to: weight Save (ix-57), hitting a streak milestone, and a day crossing 'on target' on the protein detail. On web, mirror with a confirming micro-animation/colour pulse. Promote the streak out of grey metadata into its own warm chip on progress-s00. Tie each to repeat behaviour: the weight-Save celebration reinforces the daily weigh-in habit that powers the adaptive TDEE.

topIssues.title: Two captures are mislabeled and there is NO weight metric-detail screen — coverage gap, not a render bug
topIssues.surface: metric-weight-s00, metric-weight-s01 (both render the CALORIES detail, not a weight detail)
topIssues.why: Forensic + source check: metric-weight-s00 and metric-weight-s01 render identical pixels to metric-calories-s00/s01 ('CALORIES THIS WEEK'). progress-metric.tsx only branches title on metric==='calories' | 'protein' | else→'Logging streak' — there is no 'weight' metric route. So the weight metric-detail surface is uncovered; the tour captured calories twice under a weight filename. Not a phantom and not a P0, but it means any 'weight detail' on the wall is unverified.
topIssues.fix: No code fix required for the mislabel itself — flag to the harness owner that the tour manifest names a weight metric-detail that doesn't exist. Separately, decide intentionally: should tapping the weight card open a weight-specific detail (parallel to calories/protein) or continue deep-linking to Weight & Trends? Today it's the latter; if so, retire the metric-weight tour entry so the wall isn't reviewed against a phantom surface.

topIssues.title: Protein's identity colour is the same hex as the app's primary blue, so the protein detail has no colour of its own
topIssues.surface: metric-protein-s00 (97g avg, 116g/104g day values all in #588CE4)
topIssues.why: Confirmed in theme.ts: MacroColors.protein = '#588CE4' = Accent.primary. On metric-protein-s00 the header 'PROTEIN CONSISTENCY', the AVG/DAY number, the ON TARGET '4/7', AND every day's gram value all render in the identical primary blue. There's no way to read 'this screen is about protein' from colour — it looks like a generic blue-accented list. By contrast carbs (#E8721E) and fat (#DF5EBC magenta) have distinct slots. The single most colour-identifiable macro detail is the one whose macro colour is invisible against the chrome.
topIssues.fix: This is a deliberate 8-slot collapse so may be defended — but on the protein DETAIL screen specifically, differentiate the data from the chrome: keep the header in the new calm near-black voice (per the header fix) so the blue is freed to mean 'protein data' rather than 'every accent', or give the protein number a tinted-background chip treatment so the macro reads as protein, not as generic accent.

topIssues.title: The over-target frame leads the whole tab with red while the actual story is positive maintenance — emotional-pull mismatch
topIssues.surface: progress-s00 (red 154% ring + 'Over target' as the first thing) vs progress-s00 card ('Maintenance held steady this week')
topIssues.why: The first thing the eye hits on the Progress tab is a red 154% ring and 'Over target' in the header position — the destructive-red ring is the documented correct mapping (NOT flagged here), but the surrounding framing leans the cold/alarming read, then the card immediately below says the opposite: 'Maintenance held steady this week… high confidence — keep going.' The data event is a positive (maintenance held, adaptive TDEE working) but the hero composition makes the user feel they failed. This is colour-as-emotion working against the product's own calm, supportive voice rule.
topIssues.fix: Keep the ring colour (carve-out). But rebalance the hero so the positive narrative leads and the over-target is contextual, not the headline — e.g. lead with the maintenance/confidence story and demote the bare '154% Over target' to a supporting stat, so warmth lands on the win the data actually represents. Body-neutral voice rule already mandates this.

reviews.signature: Cropped of the logo, this group does NOT confidently say "Suppr" — and worse, it doesn't even say one product. Two sibling detail screens reached by one tap from Today (macro-detail and meal-nutrition) run on two different header systems, two different icon sets, two different title treatments, and two different button-shape languages. The one genuinely strong Suppr idea present — the macro colour as the page's identity colour (carbs=amber, fat=magenta, fiber/protein each their token, applied to the value pill) — is the right instinct but it's executed by also tinting the primary CTA the full saturated macro colour, which turns a calm data screen into a loud one-colour wash and breaks the project-wide "Accent.primary is the action colour" rule. The empty/error states are clean and have a recovery CTA (good — these were dead ends before), but they read as generic centered-flat layouts any starter template would ship: no depth, no motion, no warmth, default Ionicons alert-circle. The captures available are all empty/error/not-found — the populated states (where this screen earns its keep) are uncovered, so the identity read is necessarily anchored on the chrome + empty layer, which is exactly the layer that drifts.
palette: macro-carbs-s00 / macro-fat-s00 / macro-fiber-s00 / macro-protein-s00: the primary CTA 'Log a meal' is filled with the FULL saturated macro colour (carbs #E8721E orange, fat #DF5EBC magenta, fiber #56A775 green, protein #588CE4 blue). Everywhere else in Suppr the primary action is Accent.primary (#588CE4). So on the Fat screen the main CTA is a loud magenta button, on Carbs it's a pumpkin-orange button — the same 'Log a meal' action wears 4 different brand colours across 4 sibling screens. The value pill (config.color + '20' tint with the macro colour as the number) is the correct, restrained use of the macro colour; the solid CTA is the over-reach. Root cause: macro-detail.tsx empty-state Pressable uses backgroundColor: config.color instead of Accent.primary.

macro-fat-s00: the magenta CTA (#DF5EBC) is the most emotionally loud element on an otherwise calm, near-empty screen — colour is pulling the eye to a button on a page whose job is to teach you about a nutrient. Colour-as-emotion is inverted here: the energy is on the wrong object.

meal-nutrition-s00 / s01: the 'Go back' CTA is Accent.primary blue (#588CE4) — correct and calm. This is the bar the macro-detail CTA should match. The drift is one-directional: meal-nutrition got it right, macro-detail did not.

Cross-surface note: carbs macro is #E8721E (amber-orange) and sodium elsewhere is orange too — the project context flags sodium=orange as intentional, but on the macro-detail CARBS screen the orange now reads as the page identity colour; fine in isolation, but worth confirming carbs-orange vs sodium-orange don't collide when both appear (not visible in these captures — populated state uncovered).
consistency: HEADER SYSTEM (root cause): macro-detail.tsx uses the canonical PushScreenHeader (lucide ChevronLeft 24px strokeWidth 1.75, left-aligned bold title, caption underneath, transparent over the page background, NO divider) — visible in all 4 macro-*-s00 captures. meal-nutrition.tsx uses the NATIVE React Navigation stack header (Ionicons chevron-back 26px, CENTERED title 'Meal nutrition', a visible rounded-rect grey fill behind the back chevron, and a hairline divider line under the bar) — visible in both meal-nutrition-s00/s01 captures. Two sibling nutrition-detail screens, one tap apart, on two different header primitives. This is the single biggest 'two teams' tell in the group.

ICON SET: macro-detail uses lucide ChevronLeft (via PushScreenHeader); meal-nutrition uses Ionicons chevron-back AND Ionicons alert-circle-outline for the error glyph. Project rule is exact lucide glyphs on mobile. The 'Meal not found' alert-circle in meal-nutrition-s00/s01 is an Ionicons default, not a lucide AlertCircle — drifts from the icon language and reads stock.

TITLE TREATMENT: macro-detail title ('Carbs'/'Fat'/'Fiber'/'Protein') renders large, bold, LEFT-aligned with a small 'Today' caption beneath. meal-nutrition title ('Meal nutrition') renders smaller, CENTERED, no caption. Same screen-class, opposite title alignment + scale.

BACK-CHEVRON HIT TARGET: macro-detail chevron sits flush at the leading edge with no chip; meal-nutrition chevron sits inside a visible pale rounded-rect background (the native header button surface). Inconsistent affordance for the identical 'go back' action.

CTA SHAPE/COLOUR: macro-detail 'Log a meal' = Radius.md (6px) corner, macro-colour fill, '#fff' text. meal-nutrition 'Go back' = Radius.md (6px) corner, Accent.primary fill, '#fff' text. Same radius (good) but colour token differs (drift, see palette).

DEPTH MODEL: both empty/error states are flat — no card, no shadow, centered text + button on bare background. Consistent with each other but flat vs the populated meal-nutrition state which DOES use bordered cards (styles.card: Radius.lg + borderWidth 1). So within meal-nutrition alone the error state and the data state use different surface languages.
depth: macro-carbs/fat/fiber/protein-s00: the empty state is a flat centered text + a flat solid button on the bare page background — zero elevation, zero card, no light source. It reads as a wireframe that got a colour fill on the button. A premium tracker (MacroFactor, Cronometer) anchors an empty macro screen with at least a soft-elevated illustrative tile or a ghosted ring so the page has a centre of gravity; here the only object with any weight is the loud CTA.

meal-nutrition-s00/s01 'Meal not found': flat Ionicons alert-circle outline (thin grey stroke, no fill, no container) floating above centered text. No depth, no tint, no branded error treatment. The populated meal-nutrition screen uses bordered cards (Radius.lg + 1px border) — but a 1px hairline border doing a card's job is itself the cheaper end of the depth spectrum vs a soft shadow. The depth model across the group is 'hairline border or nothing'.

The value pill on macro-detail (config.color + '20' tint, Radius.sm=4) is the one crafted micro-surface in the group — tinted background, coloured tabular number. It's the right instinct and should set the tone for the rest of the page's surfaces.

Radius incoherence at token level: Radius.sm=4, md=6, lg=8 — these are unusually TIGHT for 2026. The value pill at 4px and the CTA at 6px read almost square-cornered next to the generous rounding (16px+) that leading trackers use on cards and pills. The whole group inherits a corner language that feels a generation behind.
reviews.motion: ["No motion is observable in any capture (static PNGs), but from source: macro-detail empty-state CTA and meal-nutrition 'Go back' CTA are plain Pressables with only an opacity/scale-free press (opacity: pressed ? 0.85 : 1). They do NOT use the canonical PressableScale primitive, so they miss the 1→0.97 scale + spring-back that the rest of the app's buttons get. Two of the most-tapped recovery buttons in the group have the cheap opacity-only press the PressableScale primitive exists specifically to kill.","No transition language tying the macro tap on Today to the macro-detail screen — the macro colour is the obvious shared element that could drive a colour-matched shared-element or hero transition (tap the magenta Fat tile → magenta carries into the Fat detail header). Currently it's a plain stack push. The macro colour is sitting right there as a ready-made motion hook and is unused.","Loading state is a bare 'Loading...' text (macro-detail) / centered ActivityIndicator (meal-nutrition) — two different loading idioms for sibling screens, and neither has a skeleton that previews the page shape, so the populated content will 'pop' in rather than resolve."]
delight: macro-detail empty-state CTA: plain Pressable, haptic ABSENT. Tapping 'Log a meal' (an intent-to-engage moment) should fire a 'selection' or 'confirm' haptic via PressableScale. It fires nothing.

meal-nutrition 'Go back' / 'Nothing in slot' / 'Meal not found' CTAs: plain Pressables, haptic ABSENT. Recovery taps are low-stakes but the canonical primitive would give them the same tactile feedback as the rest of the app; right now they feel dead under the thumb.

No win-moment anywhere in this group, and that's a missed opportunity rather than a bug: the populated macro-detail page is where a user sees 'protein 142g across 4 meals' — hitting a macro target is a natural success moment that currently has no colour pulse, no haptic, no celebration. (Populated state uncovered in captures — flag for the populated-state review.)

Web parity note: macro-detail + meal-nutrition equivalents on web get no haptic (correct — web has none) but should get the motion/colour analog for the same CTAs (a 150-200ms confirming micro-animation on the primary button) so the press lands on both platforms; neither the mobile haptic nor a web analog exists today.

The error/empty copy is genuinely good and on-voice ('We couldn't find that meal. It may have been deleted or the link is missing an id.' / 'No meals logged for this day') — calm, plain English, no shaming. This is a real Suppr strength; keep it. The delight gap is tactile/visual, not verbal.

topIssues.title: Two sibling nutrition-detail screens run on two different header systems + icon sets
topIssues.surface: macro-detail (PushScreenHeader / lucide ChevronLeft, left-aligned title) vs meal-nutrition (native stack header / Ionicons chevron-back, centered title, divider) — macro-carbs-s00 & meal-nutrition-s00
topIssues.why: These two screens are one tap apart off Today and are the same screen-class (a nutrition drill-down). Cropping the logo, the header chrome alone reads as two different apps: left-aligned bold lucide header vs centered native Ionicons header with a chevron-in-a-chip and a hairline divider. This is the group's single clearest 'pile of screens that share a logo' tell.
topIssues.fix: Migrate meal-nutrition.tsx onto PushScreenHeader (it already exists and macro-detail already uses it). Drop the native stack header for the non-slot-aggregate branch, set headerShown:false, render PushScreenHeader with title=meal name, caption=date, lucide ChevronLeft, and the value pill in rightSlot — exactly as the isSlotAggregate branch already does. Owner: ui-product-designer to spec, executor to apply, behind isFeatureEnabled. Also swap the Ionicons alert-circle-outline error glyph to lucide AlertCircle per the exact-icon rule.

topIssues.title: Primary CTA is tinted the full saturated macro colour, breaking the app-wide action-colour rule
topIssues.surface: macro-detail empty state — macro-fat-s00 (magenta), macro-carbs-s00 (orange), macro-fiber-s00 (green), macro-protein-s00 (blue)
topIssues.why: Suppr's action colour is Accent.primary (#588CE4) everywhere. Here the 'Log a meal' button is filled with the page's macro colour, so the identical action wears 4 different brand colours across 4 sibling screens and the loudest object on a calm data page is a magenta/orange button. The sibling meal-nutrition 'Go back' CTA correctly uses Accent.primary — so this is one-directional drift, not a deliberate language.
topIssues.fix: In macro-detail.tsx change the empty-state Pressable backgroundColor from config.color to Accent.primary. Keep the macro colour where it belongs and reads premium: the value pill (config.color + '20' tint + coloured number) and the breakdown bar. One token swap; moves the whole group toward one action colour. Owner: design-system-enforcer / executor, behind flag.

topIssues.title: Most-tapped recovery CTAs use plain Pressables — no PressableScale, no haptic, opacity-only press
topIssues.surface: macro-detail 'Log a meal' + meal-nutrition 'Go back'/'Nothing in slot' — all four macro-*-s00 and meal-nutrition-s00/s01
topIssues.why: The PressableScale primitive exists specifically to kill opacity-only press and give every button the 1→0.97 spring + haptic. These CTAs bypass it (opacity: pressed?0.85:1, no haptic prop), so the buttons feel dead under the thumb and inconsistent with the rest of the app's tactile language. On the empty-state 'Log a meal' (an engage-intent tap) the missing 'selection'/'confirm' haptic is a real lost micro-moment.
topIssues.fix: Replace these raw Pressables with PressableScale haptic="confirm" (CTA) / haptic="selection" (recovery). On web, wire the equivalent 150-200ms confirming micro-animation on the same buttons so the press lands on both platforms. Owner: ui-product-designer + executor.

topIssues.title: Flat, stock empty/error states with no depth, default icon, and a generation-old corner radius
topIssues.surface: all four macro-*-s00 empty states + meal-nutrition-s00/s01 'Meal not found'
topIssues.why: Centered text + flat button + thin default Ionicons alert glyph on a bare background reads as a starter template, not a crafted product. The token radii (sm=4 / md=6 / lg=8) are unusually tight for 2026 and make the CTA + value pill read near-square next to the 16px+ rounding leading trackers use. No card, no soft elevation, no tint anchor — the page has no centre of gravity except the (mis-coloured) button.
topIssues.fix: Give the empty/error states a soft-elevated anchor tile (the macro colour at low alpha as a tint, a lucide glyph, generous rounding) instead of floating centered text. Revisit the radius scale toward 12-16px on pills/cards as a system token bump (affects far more than this group — route as a design-system token decision, not a per-screen patch). Owner: ui-product-designer to design the empty/error template; design-system-enforcer for the radius token call; both flag-gated.

topIssues.title: Loading and surface idioms diverge within and across the two screens
topIssues.surface: macro-detail ('Loading...' text) vs meal-nutrition (centered ActivityIndicator); meal-nutrition error (flat) vs data (bordered cards)
topIssues.why: Sibling screens use two different loading idioms (bare text vs spinner) and neither previews the page shape, so populated content pops in. Within meal-nutrition, the error state is flat while the data state uses 1px-bordered cards — two surface languages on one screen. Minor individually but it compounds the 'assembled, not designed' read.
topIssues.fix: Standardise on one loading idiom (skeleton that previews the macro-detail list / meal card shape) shared by both screens, and align the error surface to the same card/tint language as the data state. Owner: ui-product-designer; executor to apply. NOTE: populated states for both screens are UNCOVERED in this capture set — request a populated-state pass before finalising the depth/win-moment direction.

reviews.signature: There IS a recognisable Suppr signature here and it carries cleanly across most of the group: a warm off-white paper background (#fbfaf6-ish), soft-blue tinted icon tiles in rounded squares, a calm centred type voice, periwinkle primary, and the macro colour quartet (protein blue / carbs orange / fat pink / fibre green) showing up consistently on Profile, Targets and Household member rows. Crop the logo off the Settings hub, Targets ring page or Household and you'd still guess "Suppr". The identity weakens in two places: (1) the depth model is intentionally flat — Elevation.card is zeroed to a no-op in theme.ts, so every card across the whole group reads as a flat fill with a hairline edge, which is a generation behind the soft-elevation language leading settings surfaces (Things, Copilot Money, Oura) use today; and (2) two surfaces break the spell entirely — Notifications renders a crash/error boundary instead of a screen, and the Settings hub leaks a dev marker ("build ?", "MARKER F50-2026-04-22") into a user-facing row. So: a real, coherent signature undermined by one dead screen, one dev-chrome leak, and a flat depth ceiling.
palette: Macro colour quartet is used with meaning and restraint across the group — Profile 'Daily Targets' (profile-s00) shows protein blue / carbs orange / fat pink correctly, and the same colours reappear on the Targets macro tiles (targets-s00/s01) and the Household member row '901 kcal · 101g protein' (household-s00). This is a genuine differentiator; do NOT sand it flat. Hold it up as the bar.

BANNED HARDCODED HEX: notifications.tsx:83 sets the error-text colour to raw '#f87171' (Tailwind red-400) — the exact hex the project context forbids ('No hardcoded Tailwind hexes #ef4444/#f87171 — use semantic tokens'). This is the only hardcoded-hex offender in the whole group (every other settings screen is clean), so it's a one-line root fix: swap to the --destructive / colors token. Cite: notifications-s00 (the red 'Render Error' chrome is the RN redbox, but the in-app error styling that ships uses this hex).

Destructive red appears correctly on the safety-floor warning ('Below 1,200 kcal' amber-orange callout, profile-s00) and the Danger zone (settings-s03/s04, more-s02) — these are legitimate destructive/warning uses, not the calorie-ring carve-out, and they're handled well (tinted background, not raw red text).

Colour-as-emotion GAP: the Health Sync and Household screens are the most emotionally inert in the group — Health Sync (health-sync-s00) is an all-grey list of empty connection toggles ('Connect to enable' x5) with no warmth on the value moment; the 'Connected' state on the Settings hub Apple Health row (settings-s02) gets a soft green tick tile, but the Health Sync page itself never rewards a successful connection with that same green. The win-moment colour exists in the system (success green) but isn't carried to where the win actually happens.
consistency: CORNER RADIUS DRIFT past the token ceiling: the Radius scale in theme.ts tops out at xl:12, but the Settings hub cards use raw borderRadius:14 (settings.tsx:216,244), Targets uses raw 14, Household uses raw 11/16/18/24, and Notifications uses raw 999. Several of these exceed the largest token. Root cause: card radius was hand-tuned per screen instead of adding a token (e.g. Radius.card:14/16) and using it everywhere. Visible as subtly different card roundness between the hub (settings-s00) and the Household invite card (household-s00).

ICON TILE TREATMENT is consistent and good — soft-blue rounded-square tiles with lucide glyphs appear identically on Settings hub (settings-s00/s01/s02), More (more-s00/s01), and Nutrition Sources (more-s00). This is a strength; the exact-icon rule is being honoured (lucide set, not SF Symbols).

HEADER PATTERN DRIFT: most sub-pages use a left chevron + bold title in the nav bar (Profile 'Profile' profile-s00, Household 'Household' household-s00, Health Sync 'Health Sync' health-sync-s00, Targets 'Daily targets' targets-s00). But Nutrition Sources (more-s00) uses a DIFFERENT header — '< Back' text label + a centred small-caps blue 'INFO' eyebrow — which matches no other screen in the group. That's a one-off header language that reads as a different app.

SECTION-LABEL CASE DRIFT: the Settings hub and More use Title Case section headers ('Membership', 'People', 'Connections', 'Display & extras'), while Household uses ALL-CAPS small grey labels ('MEMBERS', 'PRIVACY', 'WHICH MEALS ARE SHARED?', 'WEEKLY PLAN') and Health Sync/Targets use Title Case. Two section-label systems in one group. Pick one (Title Case is the dominant, calmer choice) and apply it.

PRIMARY BUTTON SHAPE is consistent (full-width periwinkle, white bold label) — Household 'Save changes' (household-s00/s01), Health Sync 'Connect Health Data' (health-sync-s01), Profile 'Save Targets' (profile-s01). Good. But the Targets 'Recalculate' button (targets-s00) is a small white outlined pill — a secondary style that's fine, just note it's the only secondary button in the group so its weight reads slightly orphaned.
depth: ROOT DEPTH PROBLEM (system-level): Elevation.card in apps/mobile/constants/theme.ts is deliberately neutralised to a no-op — shadowColor:'transparent', shadowOpacity:0, shadowRadius:0, elevation:0. So EVERY card across the entire settings group is a flat fill distinguished from the paper background only by a hairline edge / slight tone shift. This is the single biggest 'Generic, not Premium' tell for the whole group and it's one token. Visible everywhere: Settings hub cards (settings-s00/s01), Household cards (household-s00), Health Sync cards (health-sync-s00), Targets cards (targets-s00). Leading settings surfaces today (Things, Copilot Money, Oura, Family) use a single soft ~6-8% shadow elevation with no hairline borders — Suppr is one generation behind here.

The Targets calorie-ring card (targets-s00) is the most crafted surface in the group — the periwinkle ring around '901 kcal/day' with the 'MAINTENANCE 1,494' detail has real hierarchy and is the closest to Flagship. It proves the team CAN do depth; it just isn't carried. Use this card's treatment as the depth bar for the rest.

STOCK-COMPONENT TELL: the toggles on Settings hub (settings-s01 'Track caffeine/alcohol/net carbs'), Household ('Share my nutrition targets', household-s00), and Health Sync (health-sync-s01 'Import meals from Health' etc.) render as default iOS grey pill switches with no brand tint in the OFF state and no custom ON colour shown. On a product with a defined periwinkle primary, the default-system switch is a wireframe-coloured-in tell. A custom switch (periwinkle ON track) would lift every settings row at once.

Household empty-state card 'Household is solo' (household-s00) is well-crafted — centred blue '+' tile, clear hierarchy, primary Invite CTA. Good empty-state model; it's the depth exception that the flat list rows below it don't match.
delight: NO WIN-MOMENT FEEDBACK on the highest-value settings actions. Connecting Apple Health (health-sync-s00) is the single biggest 'win' in this whole group — it's the moment a user's data comes alive — and there's no evidence of a success haptic + green-confirm celebration on it. The system has success green (used as a tick on the hub's 'Connected' row, settings-s02) but the connect action itself on the Health Sync page should fire PressableScale haptic='success' + animate the row to the green Connected state. Right now it's a silent grey toggle flip. This reinforces the connect-and-trust behaviour and is the best delight ROI in the group.

Saving (Household 'Save changes', Profile 'Save Targets', Health Sync 'Connect Health Data') are all commit-actions that should each get a haptic='confirm' on press. No evidence any of them do. These are the moments that make settings feel responsive vs inert.
