The non-negotiable: every motion path must honour prefers-reduced-motion — canvas-confetti's disableForReducedMotion, and @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) collapsing transitions to ~0.01ms with a static success state substituted. On mobile the parallel gate is AccessibilityInfo / isReduceMotionEnabled, which Suppr already wires (NorthStarBlock reduceMotion guard).

5) Multi-sensory but layered & optional. Copilot Money is the 2026 benchmark for "every interaction feels premium" — haptics + whimsy on routine actions, described as the most beautifully designed finance app where regular use is "a joy." The layering rule (HIG Feedback, 2026): haptic + visual + (optional) sound should REINFORCE one event, never substitute for each other, and the loud channels (sound, confetti) stay opt-in / landmark-only so they never hijack attention.

examples.what: Reserves loud celebration for landmark streak days (7/30/100/365) with confetti + distinct sound + haptic + a custom animated reveal; redesigning the phoenix streak animation alone moved day-7 retention +1.7%. The lesson is restraint: most apps celebrate every action identically, which dilutes the signal — Duolingo saves the big moment for landmarks.

Treats goal-completion as interrupt-worthy: hitting your nutrition goal launches an interactive mini-game (head into space, clear the meteor storm, stay in orbit). Watch app ships voice logging + haptics + complications. The 'win moment' is a genuine, reserved reward, not a toast.

Log-confirmation/suggestion card eases in over ~200ms with a small upward translate so the result lands as a 'deliberate moment, not a pop-in' — the canonical sub-300ms confirm-flourish envelope. Suppr already mirrors this at 220ms on NorthStarBlock.

Benchmark for 'every interaction feels premium' — nice haptic feedback plus a level of whimsy that makes routine budgeting 'a joy'; reviewers call it the most beautifully designed finance app, every screen and interaction premium. Proof that micro-delight on ordinary actions (not just wins) is a retention lever.

Distinct haptic per gesture (swipe-reply vs swipe-read vs long-press-react) so the thumb identifies the action before the eyes confirm — the reference for a GRADED haptic vocabulary rather than one buzz for everything.

Codifies the vocabulary: light/medium/heavy impact, selection tick, success/warning/error notification — intensity should scale with action importance; system apps play success/failure haptics consistently and many users rely on them non-visually (accessibility). .sensoryFeedback() made graded haptics declarative.

Haptics reinforce the 'glassy' physical feel; specular highlights move with device motion. Raises the ambient bar — feedback is expected to feel material and motion-responsive, but NN/g (2026) cautions Liquid Glass overuse hurts usability, reinforcing restraint.

Refreshed profile puts achievements/badges and streak counters (sleep quality, green-recovery days, double-digit strain) front and centre — landmark/streak recognition as a first-class, visible reward surface, not a buried stat.

The web win-moment toolkit: <100 particles for 60fps, GPU transforms only, and disableForReducedMotion / prefers-reduced-motion gating with a static success state substituted — the accessible web analog to a success haptic.

suprMoves: Build ONE reserved win-moment for the daily loop and gate it to landmarks only — closing the day at/under target, hitting a macro target, and streak landmarks (7/30/100). Right now Suppr fires Haptics.notificationAsync(Success) on cook-completion and one LogSheet path but has NO celebratory moment for the core 'I hit my day' win and no confetti anywhere (grep found celebration/confetti only in skeletons and paywall, not the Today loop). This is the biggest gap vs Duolingo/MacroFactor. Mobile: success haptic + a sub-300ms ring-fill-to-green spring + a one-shot lightweight confetti burst, gated behind the existing reduceMotion flag. Web: calorie ring flips success-green + canvas-confetti with disableForReducedMotion. Reserve it — do NOT fire it on every food log.

Grade the haptic vocabulary instead of the current flat usage. Audit shows Suppr leans heavily on selectionAsync + ImpactFeedbackStyle.Light/Medium fairly interchangeably across LogSheet/NorthStarBlock/cook. Map deliberately: row-select/tab = selectionAsync (tick); committing a log entry = ImpactFeedbackStyle.Light + a visual row settle; closing the day / hitting target = notificationAsync(Success) (currently underused for the headline win); destructive delete = ImpactFeedbackStyle.Heavy or notificationAsync(Warning). One weight per importance tier, consistently, so the thumb learns the language (Telegram/HIG bar).

Add a quiet 'log committed' confirm that is NOT the win moment. Every food log should get an instant (<100ms) light haptic + a brief row flash/settle + the macro tile ticking up — the Copilot-Money 'every interaction feels premium' standard for the routine action — while keeping the loud channel (confetti/sound) strictly for landmarks. This is the restraint axis: make the common case feel crisp, make the rare case feel special.

Centralise a tiny feedback helper (e.g. lib/feedback.ts) shared by web + mobile so the vocabulary can't drift between platforms — a semantic API like feedback.commit(), feedback.select(), feedback.win() that internally maps to expo-haptics on mobile and to spring+colour(+confetti) on web, each already wrapped in the reduce-motion / EXPO_OS==='ios' guards Suppr uses today. This also satisfies the CLAUDE.md web/mobile-parity rule, which a per-call-site haptic approach currently risks violating.

Hold every loud path to the 100ms / 300ms budget and make it opt-out-able. Confirm feedback must land <100ms; the win flourish must finish <300ms (MacroFactor's mini-game is the deliberate exception — an interrupt the user can dismiss, not a blocking animation). Add a Settings toggle for celebration/sound (sound and confetti opt-in per the 2026 restraint guidance), and verify the prefers-reduced-motion / AccessibilityInfo path substitutes a STATIC success state (green ring + 'Day complete' copy) rather than just removing motion — Suppr already has the reduceMotion plumbing in NorthStarBlock to extend.

Gate it behind a feature flag and prove it on pixels before ramp. Per CLAUDE.md, a win-moment is a visual/structural change — ship behind isFeatureEnabled, leave the quiet-confirm path alive in the else, and validate in sim + on web with captured frames (the 100ms/300ms timings and the green-ring success state) before ramping via PostHog. Instrument a 'day_closed_celebration_shown' event so the retention lift (Duolingo's +1.7% precedent) is measurable, not assumed.
